steps.title: Parse the equation structure
steps.content: The equation is $0.3t + 3 = 5$, where $t$ is time in months. The number 3 is a starting length (3 inches), and 5 is the target length (5 inches). The term $0.3t$ represents total growth over $t$ months.

steps.title: Identify the role of 0.3
steps.content: In a linear equation of the form $\text{rate} \times \text{time} + \text{start} = \text{end}$, the coefficient of $t$ is the rate. Here $0.3$ multiplies $t$ (months), so it represents the amount, in inches, that Antwon's hair grows each month.

steps.title: Evaluate each answer choice
steps.content:
• Choice A: "The time, in months, it will take Antwon's hair to grow 1 inch" — 0.3 is a coefficient of $t$, not itself a time value. Eliminated.
  • Choice B: "The length, in inches, Antwon's hair will grow each month" — matches the rate interpretation of the coefficient. Kept.
  • Choice C: "The time, in months, it will take Antwon's hair to reach a length of 5 inches" — that is what $t$ represents, not 0.3. Eliminated.
  • Choice D: "The length, in inches, Antwon's hair will grow in 5 months" — growth in 5 months would be $0.3 \times 5 = 1.5$, not 0.3. Eliminated.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
